sentences.text: Una dintre cele mai stranii aventuri pe care le-am trăit cu Poirot
sentences.translationRu: Одно из самых странных приключений, что я пережил с Пуаро,
sentences.original:
I have always considered that one of the most thrilling and dramatic
 of the many adventures I have shared with Poirot was that of our
 investigation into

sentences.text: a fost o serie de morți ciudate.
sentences.translationRu: была серия странных смертей.
sentences.original:
the strange series of deaths which followed upon
 the discovery and opening of the Tomb of King Men-her-Ra.

sentences.text: Totul a început în Egipt, nu departe de Cairo.
sentences.translationRu: Всё началось в Египте, недалеко от Каира.
sentences.original:
Hard upon the discovery of the Tomb of Tut-ankh-Amen by Lord
 Carnarvon, Sir John Willard and Mr. Bleibner of New York, pursuing
 their excavations not far from Cairo, in the vicinity of the
 Pyramids of Gizeh,

sentences.text: Niște oameni de știință săpau în nisip.
sentences.translationRu: Несколько учёных копали в песке.
sentences.original:
came unexpectedly on a series of funeral
 chambers. The greatest interest was aroused by their discovery.

sentences.text: Căutau morminte vechi de mii de ani.
sentences.translationRu: Они искали гробницы тысячелетней давности.

sentences.text: Într-o zi au găsit mormântul unui rege, Men-her-Ra.
sentences.translationRu: Однажды они нашли гробницу царя — Менхер-Ра.
sentences.original: The Tomb appeared to be that of King Men-her-Ra,

sentences.text: El a trăit acum foarte mult timp.
sentences.translationRu: Он жил очень давно.
sentences.original:
one of those
 shadowy kings of the Eighth Dynasty, when the Old Kingdom was
 falling to decay.

sentences.text: Ziarele au scris mult despre descoperire.
sentences.translationRu: Газеты много писали об открытии.
sentences.original:
Little was known about this period, and the
 discoveries were fully reported in the newspapers.
 
 An event soon occurred which took a profound hold on the public
 mind.

sentences.text: Curând, Sir John Willard a murit deodată.
sentences.translationRu: Вскоре сэр Джон Уиллард внезапно умер.
sentences.original: Sir John Willard died quite suddenly

sentences.text: Inima lui s-a oprit.
sentences.translationRu: Его сердце остановилось.
sentences.original: of heart failure.

sentences.text: Ziarele au început să scrie despre un blestem.
sentences.translationRu: Газеты стали писать о проклятии.
sentences.original:
The more sensational newspapers immediately took the opportunity
 of reviving all the old superstitious stories connected with

sentences.text: Ele spuneau că regele mort se răzbună.
sentences.translationRu: Они говорили, что мёртвый царь мстит.
sentences.original:
the
 ill luck of certain Egyptian treasures. The unlucky Mummy at the
 British Museum, that hoary old chestnut, was dragged out with fresh
 zest, was quietly denied by the Museum, but nevertheless enjoyed
 all its usual vogue.

sentences.text: Două săptămâni mai târziu a murit și domnul Bleibner.
sentences.translationRu: Две недели спустя умер и мистер Блайбнер.
sentences.original: A fortnight later Mr. Bleibner died of acute blood poisoning,

sentences.text: El era un om bogat și dăduse bani pentru săpături.
sentences.translationRu: Он был богат и давал деньги на раскопки.

sentences.text: Câteva zile după aceea, nepotul lui a murit la New York.
sentences.translationRu: Несколько дней спустя его племянник умер в Нью-Йорке.
sentences.original:
and
 a few days afterwards a nephew of his shot himself in New York.

sentences.text: Toată lumea vorbea despre blestemul lui Men-her-Ra.
sentences.translationRu: Все говорили о проклятии Менхер-Ра.
sentences.original:
The “Curse of Men-her-Ra” was the talk of the day, and the magic
 power of dead and gone Egypt was exalted to a fetish point.

sentences.text: Atunci, doamna Willard l-a chemat pe Poirot.
sentences.translationRu: Тогда госпожа Уиллард позвала Пуаро.
sentences.original: It was then that Poirot received a brief note from Lady Willard,

sentences.text: Era văduva omului de știință mort.
sentences.translationRu: Она была вдовой умершего учёного.
sentences.original:
widow of the dead archaeologist, asking him to go and see her at
 her house in Kensington Square.

sentences.text: Eu, Hastings, am mers cu Poirot.
sentences.translationRu: Я, Гастингс, пошёл с Пуаро.
sentences.original: I accompanied him.

sentences.text: Doamna era înaltă și slabă, în haine negre de doliu.
sentences.translationRu: Госпожа была высокой и худой, в чёрных траурных одеждах.
sentences.original: Lady Willard was a tall, thin woman, dressed in deep mourning.

sentences.text: Pe față se vedea durerea ei.
sentences.translationRu: На её лице читалось горе.
sentences.original:
Her
 haggard face bore eloquent testimony to her recent grief.

sentences.text: Ce credeți despre lucrurile supranaturale? întrebă ea.
sentences.translationRu: Что вы думаете о сверхъестественном? — спросила она.
sentences.original:
“It is kind of you to have come so promptly, Monsieur Poirot.”
 
 “I am at your service, Lady Willard. You wished to consult me?”
 
 “You are, I am aware, a detective, but it is not only as a detective
 that I wish to consult you. You are a man of original views, I
 know, you have imagination, experience of the world, tell me,
 Monsieur Poirot, what are your views on the supernatural?”
 
 Poirot hesitated for a moment before he replied. He seemed to be
 considering. Finally he said:
 
 “Let us not misunderstand each other, Lady Willard. It is not a
 general question that you are asking me there. It has a personal
 application, has it not? You are referring obliquely to the death
 of your late husband?”
 
 “That is so,” she admitted.
 
 “You want me to investigate the circumstances of his death?”
 
 “I want you to ascertain for me exactly how much is newspaper
 chatter, and how much may be said to be founded on fact?

sentences.text: Trei oameni au murit deja, toți într-o singură lună.
sentences.translationRu: Уже умерли трое, все за один месяц.
sentences.original:
Three
 deaths, Monsieur Poirot—each one explicable taken by itself, but
 taken together surely an almost unbelievable coincidence, and all
 within a month of the opening of the tomb! It may be mere
 superstition, it may be some potent curse from the past that
 operates in ways undreamed of by modern science. The fact
 remains—three deaths!

sentences.text: Mi-e foarte frică, spuse ea.
sentences.translationRu: Мне очень страшно, — сказала она.
sentences.original:
And I am afraid, Monsieur Poirot, horribly
 afraid. It may not yet be the end.”
 
 “For whom do you fear?”

sentences.text: Pentru cine vă temeți? întrebă Poirot.
sentences.translationRu: За кого вы боитесь? — спросил Пуаро.

sentences.text: Pentru fiul meu, răspunse ea.
sentences.translationRu: За моего сына, — ответила она.
sentences.original: “For my son.

sentences.text: El a adus corpul tatălui acasă, dar acum s-a întors în Egipt.
sentences.translationRu: Он привёз тело отца домой, но теперь вернулся в Египет.
sentences.original:
When the news of my husband’s death came I was ill.
 My son, who has just come down from Oxford, went out there. He
 brought the—the body home, but now he has gone out again, in spite
 of my prayers and entreaties.

sentences.text: El vrea să continue munca tatălui.
sentences.translationRu: Он хочет продолжить дело отца.
sentences.original:
He is so fascinated by the work that
 he intends to take his father’s place and carry on the system of
 excavations. You may think me a foolish, credulous woman, but,
 Monsieur Poirot, I am afraid.

sentences.text: Mă tem că regele mort nu este liniștit.
sentences.translationRu: Боюсь, что мёртвый царь не успокоился.
sentences.original:
Supposing that the spirit of the dead
 King is not yet appeased? Perhaps to you I seem to be talking
 nonsense——”
 
 “No, indeed, Lady Willard,” said Poirot quickly. “I, too, believe
 in the force of superstition, one of the greatest forces the world
 has ever known.”
 
 I looked at him in surprise. I should never have credited Poirot
 with being superstitious. But the little man was obviously in
 earnest.
 
 “What you really demand is that I shall protect your son?

sentences.text: Voi face tot posibilul să vă protejez fiul, spuse Poirot.
sentences.translationRu: Я сделаю всё возможное, чтобы защитить вашего сына, — сказал Пуаро.
sentences.original:
I will
 do my utmost to keep him from harm.”
 
 “Yes, in the ordinary way, but against an occult influence?”
 
 “In volumes of the Middle Ages, Lady Willard, you will find many
 ways of counteracting black magic. Perhaps they knew more than we
 moderns with all our boasted science. Now let us come to facts,
 that I may have guidance. Your husband had always been a devoted
 Egyptologist, hadn’t he?”
 
 “Yes, from his youth upwards. He was one of the greatest living
 authorities upon the subject.”
 
 “But Mr. Bleibner, I understand, was more or less of an amateur?”
 
 “Oh, quite. He was a very wealthy man who dabbled freely in any
 subject that happened to take his fancy. My husband managed to
 interest him in Egyptology, and it was his money that was so useful
 in financing the expedition.”
 
 “And the nephew? What do you know of his tastes? Was he with the
 party at all?”
 
 “I do not think so. In fact I never knew of his existence till I
 read of his death in the paper, I do not think he and Mr. Bleibner
 can have been at all intimate. He never spoke of having any
 relations.”

sentences.text: Spuneți-mi, cine mai este la săpături?
sentences.translationRu: Скажите, кто ещё на раскопках?
sentences.original: “Who are the other members of the party?”

sentences.text: Doctorul Tosswill, de la un muzeu.
sentences.translationRu: Доктор Тоссуилл, из музея.
sentences.original:
“Well, there is Dr. Tosswill, a minor official connected with the
 British Museum;

sentences.text: Domnul Schneider, din New York.
sentences.translationRu: Мистер Шнайдер, из Нью-Йорка.
sentences.original:
Mr. Schneider of the Metropolitan Museum in New
 York;

sentences.text: Secretarul Harper și doctorul Ames, medicul grupului.
sentences.translationRu: Секретарь Харпер и доктор Эймс, врач группы.
sentences.original:
a young American secretary; Dr. Ames, who accompanies the
 expedition in his professional capacity;

sentences.text: Și Hassan, un servitor fidel.
sentences.translationRu: И Хассан, верный слуга.
sentences.original:
and Hassan, my husband’s
 devoted native servant.”
 
 “Do you remember the name of the American secretary?”
 
 “Harper, I think, but I cannot be sure. He had not been with Mr.
 Bleibner very long, I know. He was a very pleasant young fellow.”
 
 “Thank you, Lady Willard.”
 
 “If there is anything else——?”
 
 “For the moment, nothing. Leave it now in my hands, and be assured
 that I will do all that is humanly possible to protect your son.”
 
 They were not exactly reassuring words, and I observed Lady Willard
 wince as he uttered them. Yet, at the same time, the fact that he
 had not pooh-poohed her fears seemed in itself to be a relief to
 her.
 
 For my part I had never before suspected that Poirot had so deep
 a vein of superstition in his nature. I tackled him on the subject
 as we went homewards. His manner was grave and earnest.
 
 “But yes, Hastings. I believe in these things. You must not
 underrate the force of superstition.”
 
 “What are we going to do about it?”

sentences.text: După vizită, Poirot a cerut vești de la New York.
sentences.translationRu: После визита Пуаро запросил вести из Нью-Йорка.
sentences.original:
“Toujours pratique, the good Hastings! Eh bien, to begin with
 we are going to cable to New York for fuller details of young Mr.
 Bleibner’s death.”
 
 He duly sent off his cable. The reply was full and precise.

sentences.text: Nepotul, Rupert Bleibner, era sărac.
sentences.translationRu: Племянник, Руперт Блайбнер, был беден.
sentences.original:
Young
 Rupert Bleibner had been in low water for several years. He had
 been a beach-comber and a remittance man in several South Sea
 islands, but had returned to New York two years ago, where he had
 rapidly sunk lower and lower.

sentences.text: El a împrumutat bani ca să meargă în Egipt.
sentences.translationRu: Он занял денег, чтобы поехать в Египет.
sentences.original:
The most significant thing, to my
 mind, was that he had recently managed to borrow enough money to
 take him to Egypt.

sentences.text: Am un prieten bun acolo, spusese el.
sentences.translationRu: У меня там есть хороший друг, — говорил он.
sentences.original:
“I’ve a good friend there I can borrow from,”
 he had declared.

sentences.text: În Egipt, planurile lui au eșuat, și s-a întors fără bani.
sentences.translationRu: В Египте его планы провалились, и он вернулся без денег.
sentences.original:
Here, however, his plans had gone awry. He had
 returned to New York cursing his skinflint of an uncle who cared
 more for the bones of dead and gone kings than his own flesh and
 blood. It was during his sojourn in Egypt that the death of Sir
 John Willard occurred. Rupert had plunged once more into his life
 of dissipation in New York, and then, without warning, he had
 committed suicide, leaving behind him a letter which contained some
 curious phrases. It seemed written in a sudden fit of remorse.

sentences.text: Apoi a scris o scrisoare ciudată: Sunt un lepros.
sentences.translationRu: Потом он написал странное письмо: Я прокажённый.
sentences.original:
He
 referred to himself as a leper and an outcast,

sentences.text: Oameni ca mine ar fi mai bine morți.
sentences.translationRu: Таким, как я, лучше умереть.
sentences.original:
and the letter ended
 by declaring that such as he were better dead.

sentences.text: Eu cred că nepotul a vrut să-și omoare unchiul, am spus.
sentences.translationRu: Я думаю, что племянник хотел убить дядю, — сказал я.
sentences.original:
A shadowy theory leapt into my brain. I had never really believed
 in the vengeance of a long dead Egyptian king. I saw here a more
 modern crime. Supposing this young man had decided to do away with
 his uncle—preferably by poison. By mistake, Sir John Willard
 receives the fatal dose. The young man returns to New York, haunted
 by his crime. The news of his uncle’s death reaches him.

sentences.text: Apoi, plin de regret, s-a omorât.
sentences.translationRu: Потом, полный раскаяния, убил себя.
sentences.original:
He realizes
 how unnecessary his crime has been, and stricken with remorse takes
 his own life.
 
 I outlined my solution to Poirot.

sentences.text: Poirot a ascultat cu interes.
sentences.translationRu: Пуаро слушал с интересом.
sentences.original:
He was interested.
 
 “It is ingenious what you have thought of there—decidedly it is
 ingenious. It may even be true. But you leave out of count the
 fatal influence of the Tomb.”
 
 I shrugged my shoulders.
 
 “You still think that has something to do with it?”
 
 “So much so, mon ami, that

sentences.text: Mergem mâine în Egipt, spuse el.
sentences.translationRu: Завтра едем в Египет, — сказал он.
sentences.original:
we start for Egypt to-morrow.”
 
 “What?” I cried, astonished.
 
 “I have said it.” An expression of conscious heroism spread over
 Poirot’s face. Then he groaned.

sentences.text: Dar, oh, marea cea urâtă! a oftat el.
sentences.translationRu: Но, ах, ненавистное море! — вздохнул он.
sentences.original:
“But, oh,” he lamented, “the sea!
 The hateful sea!”
 
 
 • • • • • • •

sentences.text: O săptămână mai târziu eram în deșert.
sentences.translationRu: Неделю спустя мы были в пустыне.
sentences.original:
It was a week later. Beneath our feet was the golden sand of the
 desert.

sentences.text: Soarele fierbinte ardea deasupra noastră.
sentences.translationRu: Жаркое солнце пекло над нами.
sentences.original: The hot sun poured down overhead.

sentences.text: Călătoria pe mare a fost grea pentru Poirot.
sentences.translationRu: Морское путешествие было тяжким для Пуаро.
sentences.original:
Poirot, the picture of
 misery, wilted by my side. The little man was not a good traveller.
 Our four days’ voyage from Marseilles had been one long agony to
 him. He had landed at Alexandria the wraith of his former self,
 even his usual neatness had deserted him. We had arrived in Cairo
 and had driven out at once to the Mena House Hotel, right in the
 shadow of the Pyramids.
 
 The charm of Egypt had laid hold of me. Not so Poirot. Dressed
 precisely the same as in London, he carried a small clothes-brush
 in his pocket and waged an unceasing war on the dust which
 accumulated on his dark apparel.
 
 “And my boots,” he wailed. “Regard them, Hastings. My boots, of
 the neat patent leather, usually so smart and shining. See,

sentences.text: Nisipul intră în pantofii lui, se plângea el.
sentences.translationRu: Песок забивается в его ботинки, — жаловался он.
sentences.original:
the
 sand is inside them, which is painful, and outside them, which
 outrages the eyesight. Also the heat, it causes my moustaches to
 become limp—but limp!”
 
 “Look at the Sphinx,” I urged. “Even I can feel the mystery and
 the charm it exhales.”
 
 Poirot looked at it discontentedly.
 
 “It has not the air happy,” he declared. “How could it, half-buried
 in sand in that untidy fashion.

sentences.text: Acest nisip blestemat! spunea Poirot.
sentences.translationRu: Этот проклятый песок! — говорил Пуаро.
sentences.original:
Ah, this cursed sand!”
 
 “Come, now, there’s a lot of sand in Belgium,” I reminded him,
 mindful of a holiday spent at Knocke-sur-mer in the midst of “les
 dunes impeccables” as the guide-book had phrased it.
 
 “Not in Brussels,” declared Poirot. He gazed at the Pyramids
 thoughtfully. “It is true that they, at least, are of a shape solid
 and geometrical, but their surface is of an unevenness most
 unpleasing. And the palm-trees I like them not. Not even do they
 plant them in rows!”
 
 I cut short his lamentations, by suggesting that we should start
 for the camp.

sentences.text: Am mers la tabără pe cămile.
sentences.translationRu: Мы ехали в лагерь на верблюдах.
sentences.original:
We were to ride there on camels, and the beasts were
 patiently kneeling, waiting for us to mount, in charge of several
 picturesque boys headed by a voluble dragoman.

sentences.text: Poirot nu a stat bine pe cămilă.
sentences.translationRu: Пуаро плохо держался на верблюде.
sentences.original:
I pass over the spectacle of Poirot on a camel. He started by
 groans and lamentations and ended by shrieks, gesticulations and
 invocations to the Virgin Mary and every Saint in the calendar. In
 the end, he descended ignominiously and

sentences.text: La final a coborât și a mers pe un măgar mic.
sentences.translationRu: В конце он слез и поехал на маленьком ослике.
sentences.original:
finished the journey on a
 diminutive donkey. I must admit that a trotting camel is no joke
 for the amateur. I was stiff for several days.
 
 At last we neared the scene of the excavations.

sentences.text: La săpături ne-a întâmpinat un om cu barbă gri.
sentences.translationRu: У раскопок нас встретил человек с седой бородой.
sentences.original:
A sunburnt man with
 a grey beard, in white clothes and wearing a helmet, came to meet
 us.

sentences.text: Numele meu este Tosswill, spuse el.
sentences.translationRu: Меня зовут Тоссуилл, — сказал он.
sentences.original:
“Monsieur Poirot and Captain Hastings? We received your cable. I’m
 sorry that there was no one to meet you in Cairo.

sentences.text: S-a întâmplat ceva neașteptat.
sentences.translationRu: Случилось нечто неожиданное.
sentences.original:
An unforeseen
 event occurred which completely disorganized our plans.”
 
 Poirot paled. His hand, which had stolen to his clothes-brush,
 stayed its course.

sentences.text: Poirot a pălit. Încă o moarte? a întrebat el.
sentences.translationRu: Пуаро побледнел. Ещё одна смерть? — спросил он.
sentences.original:
“Not another death?” he breathed.
 
 “Yes.”

sentences.text: Sir Guy Willard? am strigat eu.
sentences.translationRu: Сэр Гай Уиллард? — крикнул я.
sentences.original: “Sir Guy Willard?” I cried.

sentences.text: Nu, spuse Tosswill. Domnul Schneider, de tetanos.
sentences.translationRu: Нет, — сказал Тоссуилл. — Мистер Шнайдер, от столбняка.
sentences.original:
“No, Captain Hastings. My American colleague, Mr. Schneider.”
 
 “And the cause?” demanded Poirot.
 
 “Tetanus.”

sentences.text: Am simțit ceva rău în aer.
sentences.translationRu: Я почувствовал в воздухе что-то злое.
sentences.original:
I blanched. All around me I seemed to feel an atmosphere of evil,
 subtle and menacing. A horrible thought flashed across me. Supposing
 I were the next?
 
 “Mon Dieu,” said Poirot, in a very low voice, “I do not understand
 this. It is horrible. Tell me, monsieur, there is no doubt that it
 was tetanus?”
 
 “I believe not. But Dr. Ames will tell you more than I can do.”
 
 “Ah, of course, you are not the doctor.”
 
 “My name is Tosswill.”
 
 This, then, was the British expert described by Lady Willard as
 being a minor official at the British Museum. There was something
 at once grave and steadfast about him that took my fancy.
 
 “If you will come with me,” continued Dr. Tosswill, “I will take
 you to Sir Guy Willard. He was most anxious to be informed as soon
 as you should arrive.”

sentences.text: Tosswill ne-a dus la un cort mare.
sentences.translationRu: Тоссуилл повёл нас к большой палатке.
sentences.original:
We were taken across the camp to a large tent. Dr. Tosswill lifted
 up the flap and we entered.

sentences.text: Înăuntru erau trei bărbați.
sentences.translationRu: Внутри были трое мужчин.
sentences.original: Three men were sitting inside.

sentences.text: Cel mai tânăr s-a ridicat repede: era Sir Guy Willard.
sentences.translationRu: Самый молодой быстро встал: это был сэр Гай Уиллард.
sentences.original:
“Monsieur Poirot and Captain Hastings have arrived, Sir Guy,” said
 Tosswill.
 
 The youngest of the three men jumped up and came forward to greet
 us. There was a certain impulsiveness in his manner which reminded
 me of his mother. He was not nearly so sunburnt as the others, and
 that fact, coupled with a certain haggardness round the eyes, made
 him look older than

sentences.text: Avea douăzeci și doi de ani.
sentences.translationRu: Ему было двадцать два года.
sentences.original:
his twenty-two years. He was clearly endeavouring
 to bear up under a severe mental strain.

sentences.text: Lângă el erau doctorul Ames și secretarul Harper.
sentences.translationRu: Рядом с ним были доктор Эймс и секретарь Харпер.
sentences.original:
He introduced his two companions, Dr. Ames, a capable looking man
 of thirty odd, with a touch of greying hair at the temples, and
 Mr. Harper, the secretary, a pleasant lean young man wearing the
 national insignia of horn-rimmed spectacles.
 
 After a few minutes’ desultory conversation the latter went out,
 and Dr. Tosswill followed him. We were left alone with Sir Guy and
 Dr. Ames.
 
 “Please ask any questions you want to ask, Monsieur Poirot,” said
 Willard. “We are utterly dumbfounded at this strange series of
 disasters, but it isn’t—it can’t be, anything but coincidence.”

sentences.text: Munca va continua, orice s-ar întâmpla, spuse Sir Guy.
sentences.translationRu: Работа продолжится, что бы ни случилось, — сказал сэр Гай.
sentences.original:
There was a nervousness about his manner which rather belied the
 words. I saw that Poirot was studying him keenly.
 
 “Your heart is really in this work, Sir Guy?”
 
 “Rather. No matter what happens, or what comes of it, the work is
 going on. Make up your mind to that.”

sentences.text: Dar vocea lui era nervoasă.
sentences.translationRu: Но голос его был тревожным.

sentences.text: Poirot îl privea cu atenție.
sentences.translationRu: Пуаро внимательно за ним наблюдал.
sentences.original:
Poirot wheeled round on the other.
 
 “What have you to say to that, monsieur le docteur?”
 
 “Well,” drawled the doctor, “I’m not for quitting myself.”
 
 Poirot made one of those expressive grimaces of his.

sentences.text: Poirot l-a întrebat pe doctorul Ames despre Schneider.
sentences.translationRu: Пуаро спросил доктора Эймса о Шнайдере.
sentences.original:
“Then, évidemment, we must find out just how we stand. When did
 Mr. Schneider’s death take place?”
 
 “Three days ago.”

sentences.text: Sunteți sigur că a fost tetanos? întrebă Poirot.
sentences.translationRu: Вы уверены, что это был столбняк? — спросил Пуаро.
sentences.original: “You are sure it was tetanus?”

sentences.text: Sunt sigur, răspunse Ames.
sentences.translationRu: Уверен, — ответил Эймс.
sentences.original: “Dead sure.”

sentences.text: Așadar, sunt patru morți, toți altfel, spuse Poirot.
sentences.translationRu: Итак, четверо мёртвых, все по-разному, — сказал Пуаро.
sentences.original:
“It couldn’t have been a case of strychnine poisoning, for
 instance?”
 
 “No, Monsieur Poirot. I see what you’re getting at. But it was a
 clear case of tetanus.”
 
 “Did you not inject anti-serum?”
 
 “Certainly we did,” said the doctor dryly. “Every conceivable thing
 that could be done was tried.”
 
 “Had you the anti-serum with you?”
 
 “No. We procured it from Cairo.”
 
 “Have there been any other cases of tetanus in the camp?”
 
 “No, not one.”
 
 “Are you certain that the death of Mr. Bleibner was not due to
 tetanus?”
 
 “Absolutely plumb certain. He had a scratch upon his thumb which
 became poisoned, and septicæmia set in. It sounds pretty much the
 same to a layman, I dare say, but the two things are entirely
 different.”
 
 “Then we have four deaths—all totally dissimilar, one heart failure,
 one blood poisoning, one suicide and one tetanus.”
 
 “Exactly, Monsieur Poirot.”

sentences.text: Există ceva ce îi leagă? întrebă el.
sentences.translationRu: Есть ли что-то, что их связывает? — спросил он.
sentences.original:
“Are you certain that there is nothing which might link the four
 together?”
 
 “I don’t quite understand you?”
 
 “I will put it plainly. Was any act committed by those four men
 which might seem to denote disrespect to the spirit of Men-her-Ra?”
 
 The doctor gazed at Poirot in astonishment.
 
 “You’re talking through your hat, Monsieur Poirot. Surely you’ve
 not been guyed into believing all that fool talk?”
 
 “Absolute nonsense,” muttered Willard angrily.
 
 Poirot remained placidly immovable, blinking a little out of his
 green cat’s eyes.
 
 “So you do not believe it, monsieur le docteur?”

sentences.text: Doctorul Ames nu credea în blestem.
sentences.translationRu: Доктор Эймс не верил в проклятие.
sentences.original: “No, sir, I do not,” declared the doctor emphatically.

sentences.text: Sunt un om de știință, spuse el.
sentences.translationRu: Я человек науки, — сказал он.
sentences.original:
“I am a
 scientific man,

sentences.text: Cred numai ce spune știința.
sentences.translationRu: Я верю лишь в то, чему учит наука.
sentences.original: and I believe only what science teaches.”

sentences.text: Poirot a zâmbit și a scos o carte veche despre magie.
sentences.translationRu: Пуаро улыбнулся и достал старую книгу о магии.
sentences.original:
“Was there no science then in Ancient Egypt?” asked Poirot softly.
 He did not wait for a reply, and indeed Dr. Ames seemed rather at
 a loss for the moment. “No, no, do not answer me, but tell me this.
 What do the native workmen think?”
 
 “I guess,” said Dr. Ames, “that, where white folk lose their heads,
 natives aren’t going to be far behind. I’ll admit that they’re
 getting what you might call scared—but they’ve no cause to be.”
 
 “I wonder,” said Poirot non-committally.
 
 Sir Guy leant forward.
 
 “Surely,” he cried incredulously, “you cannot believe in—oh, but
 the thing’s absurd! You can know nothing of Ancient Egypt if you
 think that.”
 
 For answer Poirot produced a little book from his pocket—an ancient
 tattered volume. As he held it out I saw its title, The Magic of
 the Egyptians and Chaldeans. Then, wheeling round, he strode out
 of the tent. The doctor stared at me.
 
 “What is his little idea?”
 
 The phrase, so familiar on Poirot’s lips, made me smile as it came
 from another.
 
 “I don’t know exactly,” I confessed. “He’s got some plan of
 exorcizing the evil spirits, I believe.”

sentences.text: Apoi Poirot a vorbit cu Harper.
sentences.translationRu: Потом Пуаро поговорил с Харпером.
sentences.original:
I went in search of Poirot, and found him talking to the lean-faced
 young man who had been the late Mr. Bleibner’s secretary.
 
 “No,” Mr. Harper was saying, “I’ve only been six months with the
 expedition. Yes, I knew Mr. Bleibner’s affairs pretty well.”

sentences.text: Spuneți-mi despre nepot, ceru el.
sentences.translationRu: Расскажите мне о племяннике, — попросил он.
sentences.original: “Can you recount to me anything concerning his nephew?”

sentences.text: A venit aici într-o zi, dar unchiul nu a fost bucuros.
sentences.translationRu: Он приехал сюда однажды, но дядя не был рад.
sentences.original:
“He turned up here one day, not a bad-looking fellow. I’d never
 met him before, but some of the others had—Ames, I think, and
 Schneider. The old man wasn’t at all pleased to see him. They were
 at it in no time, hammer and tongs.

sentences.text: Niciun ban! a strigat unchiul.
sentences.translationRu: Ни гроша! — крикнул дядя.
sentences.original:
‘Not a cent,’ the old man
 shouted. ‘Not one cent now or when I’m dead.

sentences.text: Vreau să las banii pentru munca mea.
sentences.translationRu: Я хочу оставить деньги на моё дело.
sentences.original:
I intend to leave my
 money to the furtherance of my life’s work. I’ve been talking it
 over with Mr. Schneider to-day.’ And a bit more of the same. Young
 Bleibner lit out for Cairo right away.”
 
 “Was he in perfectly good health at the time?”
 
 “The old man?”

sentences.text: Cunoșteați pe nepot? întrebă Poirot.
sentences.translationRu: Вы знали племянника? — спросил Пуаро.
sentences.original:
“No, the young one.”
 
 “I believe he did mention there was something wrong with him. But
 it couldn’t have been anything serious, or I should have remembered.”

sentences.text: Eu nu, dar Ames și Schneider, da, spuse Harper.
sentences.translationRu: Я нет, но Эймс и Шнайдер — да, — сказал Харпер.

sentences.text: Bleibner a lăsat un testament? întrebă Poirot.
sentences.translationRu: Блайбнер оставил завещание? — спросил Пуаро.
sentences.original: “One thing more, has Mr. Bleibner left a will?”

sentences.text: Nu știm de niciunul, spuse Harper.
sentences.translationRu: Нам ни об одном неизвестно, — сказал Харпер.
sentences.original:
“So far as we know, he has not.”
 
 “Are you remaining with the expedition, Mr. Harper?”

sentences.text: Eu plec. Nu vreau să fiu următorul.
sentences.translationRu: Я уезжаю. Не хочу быть следующим.
sentences.original:
“No, sir, I am not. I’m for New York as soon as I can square up
 things here. You may laugh if you like, but I’m not going to be
 this blasted old Men-her-Ra’s next victim. He’ll get me if I stop
 here.”
 
 The young man wiped the perspiration from his brow.

sentences.text: Poirot a zâmbit ciudat.
sentences.translationRu: Пуаро странно улыбнулся.
sentences.original:
Poirot turned away. Over his shoulder he said with a peculiar
 smile:
 
 “Remember, he got one of his victims in New York.”
 
 “Oh, hell!” said Mr. Harper forcibly.

sentences.text: Acel tânăr este foarte nervos, spuse el.
sentences.translationRu: Этот молодой человек очень напряжён, — сказал он.
sentences.original:
“That young man is nervous,” said Poirot thoughtfully. “He is on
 the edge, but absolutely on the edge.”
 
 I glanced at Poirot curiously, but his enigmatical smile told me
 nothing. In company with Sir Guy Willard and Dr. Tosswill we were
 taken round the excavations. The principal finds had been removed
 to Cairo, but some of the tomb furniture was extremely interesting.
 The enthusiasm of the young baronet was obvious, but I fancied that
 I detected a shade of nervousness in his manner as though he could
 not quite escape from the feeling of menace in the air.

sentences.text: Lângă cortul nostru stătea Hassan.
sentences.translationRu: У нашей палатки стоял Хассан.
sentences.original:
As we
 entered the tent which had been assigned to us, for a wash before
 joining the evening meal, a tall dark figure in white robes stood
 aside to let us pass with a graceful gesture and a murmured greeting
 in Arabic. Poirot stopped.
 
 “You are Hassan, the late Sir John Willard’s servant?”
 
 “I served my Lord Sir John, now I serve his son.”

sentences.text: El a vorbit încet: Lăsați pe tânărul stăpân să plece.
sentences.translationRu: Он тихо заговорил: Пусть молодой господин уедет.
sentences.original:
He took a step
 nearer to us and lowered his voice. “You are a wise one, they say,
 learned in dealing with evil spirits. Let the young master depart
 from here.

sentences.text: Este ceva rău în aer, spuse el.
sentences.translationRu: В воздухе что-то злое, — сказал он.
sentences.original:
There is evil in the air around us.”
 
 And with an abrupt gesture, not waiting for a reply, he strode
 away.
 
 “Evil in the air,” muttered Poirot. “Yes, I feel it.”
 
 Our meal was hardly a cheerful one. The floor was left to Dr.
 Tosswill, who discoursed at length upon Egyptian antiquities. Just
 as we were preparing to retire to rest, Sir Guy caught Poirot by
 the arm and pointed.

sentences.text: Seara, am văzut o umbră printre corturi.
sentences.translationRu: Вечером мы увидели тень меж палаток.
sentences.original: A shadowy figure was moving amidst the tents.

sentences.text: Nu era un om: avea cap de câine.
sentences.translationRu: Это был не человек: у неё была голова собаки.
sentences.original:
It was no human one: I recognized distinctly the dog-headed figure
 I had seen carved on the walls of the tomb.
 
 My blood literally froze at the sight.

sentences.text: Era Anubis, un vechi zeu egiptean.
sentences.translationRu: Это был Анубис, древний египетский бог.
sentences.original:
“Mon Dieu!” murmured Poirot, crossing himself vigorously. “Anubis,
 the jackal-headed, the god of departing souls.”

sentences.text: Umbra a intrat în cortul lui Ames.
sentences.translationRu: Тень вошла в палатку Эймса.
sentences.original:
“Some one is hoaxing us,” cried Dr. Tosswill, rising indignantly
 to his feet.
 
 “It went into your tent, Harper,” muttered Sir Guy, his face
 dreadfully pale.
 
 “No,” said Poirot, shaking his head, “into that of the Dr. Ames.”
 
 The doctor stared at him incredulously; then, repeating Dr.
 Tosswill’s words, he cried:

sentences.text: Cineva ne face o farsă! strigă Tosswill.
sentences.translationRu: Кто-то нас разыгрывает! — крикнул Тоссуилл.
sentences.original: “Some one is hoaxing us. Come, we’ll soon catch the fellow.”

sentences.text: Ames a fugit după umbră, dar nu am găsit nimic.
sentences.translationRu: Эймс побежал за тенью, но мы ничего не нашли.
sentences.original:
He dashed energetically in pursuit of the shadowy apparition. I
 followed him, but, search as we would, we could find no trace of
 any living soul having passed that way.

sentences.text: Poirot a desenat semne în nisip ca să ne apere de spirite.
sentences.translationRu: Пуаро рисовал знаки на песке, чтобы защитить нас от духов.
sentences.original:
We returned, somewhat
 disturbed in mind, to find Poirot taking energetic measures, in
 his own way, to ensure his personal safety. He was busily
 surrounding our tent with various diagrams and inscriptions which
 he was drawing in the sand. I recognized the five-pointed star or
 Pentagon many times repeated. As was his wont, Poirot was at the
 same time delivering an impromptu lecture on witchcraft and magic
 in general, White Magic as opposed to Black, with various references
 to the Ka and the Book of the Dead thrown in.
 
 It appeared to excite the liveliest contempt in Dr. Tosswill, who
 drew me aside, literally snorting with rage.
 
 “Balderdash, sir,” he exclaimed angrily. “Pure balderdash. The
 man’s an impostor. He doesn’t know the difference between the
 superstitions of the Middle Ages and the beliefs of Ancient Egypt.
 Never have I heard such a hotch-potch of ignorance and credulity.”
 
 I calmed the excited expert, and joined Poirot in the tent. My
 little friend was beaming cheerfully.

sentences.text: Acum putem dormi liniștiți, spuse Poirot.
sentences.translationRu: Теперь мы можем спать спокойно, — сказал Пуаро.
sentences.original:
“We can now sleep in peace,” he declared happily. “And I can do
 with some sleep.

sentences.text: Dar capul mă doare tare.
sentences.translationRu: Но голова сильно болит.
sentences.original:
My head, it aches abominably. Ah, for a good
 tisane!”

sentences.text: Atunci Hassan a adus un ceai cald de mușețel.
sentences.translationRu: Тогда Хассан принёс тёплый ромашковый чай.
sentences.original:
As though in answer to prayer, the flap of the tent was lifted and
 Hassan appeared, bearing a steaming cup which he offered to Poirot.
 It proved to be camomile tea,

sentences.text: Poirot iubește acest ceai.
sentences.translationRu: Пуаро любит этот чай.
sentences.original:
a beverage of which he is inordinately
 fond. Having thanked Hassan and refused his offer of another cup
 for myself, we were left alone once more.

sentences.text: Eu stăteam la ușa cortului.
sentences.translationRu: Я стоял у входа в палатку.
sentences.original:
I stood at the door of
 the tent some time after undressing, looking out over the desert.

sentences.text: Ce loc minunat! am spus eu.
sentences.translationRu: Какое чудесное место! — сказал я.
sentences.original:
“A wonderful place,” I said aloud, “and a wonderful work. I can
 feel the fascination. This desert life, this probing into the heart
 of a vanished civilization. Surely, Poirot, you, too, must feel
 the charm?”

sentences.text: Nu am primit niciun răspuns și m-am întors.
sentences.translationRu: Я не получил ответа и обернулся.
sentences.original:
I got no answer, and I turned, a little annoyed. My annoyance was
 quickly changed to concern.

sentences.text: Poirot stătea căzut pe pat, cu fața schimonosită.
sentences.translationRu: Пуаро лежал на кровати, с искажённым лицом.
sentences.original:
Poirot was lying back across the rude
 couch, his face horribly convulsed.

sentences.text: Lângă el era cupa goală.
sentences.translationRu: Рядом с ним была пустая чашка.
sentences.original: Beside him was the empty cup.

sentences.text: Am fugit la cortul doctorului Ames.
sentences.translationRu: Я побежал к палатке доктора Эймса.
sentences.original:
I rushed to his side, then dashed out and across the camp to Dr.
 Ames’s tent.
 
 “Dr. Ames!” I cried. “Come at once.”
 
 “What’s the matter?” said the doctor, appearing in pyjamas.

sentences.text: Veniți repede! Prietenul meu moare. A băut ceaiul.
sentences.translationRu: Идите скорее! Мой друг умирает. Он выпил чай.
sentences.original: “My friend. He’s ill. Dying. The camomile tea.

sentences.text: Nu lăsați pe Hassan să plece din tabără.
sentences.translationRu: Не дайте Хассану уйти из лагеря.
sentences.original:
Don’t let Hassan
 leave the camp.”

sentences.text: Doctorul a venit repede și a luat cupa goală.
sentences.translationRu: Доктор быстро пришёл и взял пустую чашку.
sentences.original:
Like a flash the doctor ran to our tent. Poirot was lying as I left
 him.
 
 “Extraordinary,” cried Ames. “Looks like a seizure—or—what did you
 say about something he drank?” He picked up the empty cup.

sentences.text: Dar eu nu am băut! spuse o voce liniștită.
sentences.translationRu: Но я не пил! — сказал спокойный голос.
sentences.original: “Only I did not drink it!” said a placid voice.

sentences.text: Poirot stătea pe pat și zâmbea.
sentences.translationRu: Пуаро сидел на кровати и улыбался.
sentences.original:
We turned in amazement. Poirot was sitting up on the bed. He was
 smiling.

sentences.text: Nu am băut ceaiul. L-am pus într-o sticluță mică.
sentences.translationRu: Я не пил чай. Я перелил его в маленькую бутылочку.
sentences.original:
“No,” he said gently. “I did not drink it. While my good friend
 Hastings was apostrophizing the night, I took the opportunity of
 pouring it, not down my throat, but into a little bottle.

sentences.text: Sticluța va merge la un chimist.
sentences.translationRu: Бутылочка отправится к химику.
sentences.original:
That
 little bottle will go to the analytical chemist.

sentences.text: Violența nu vă va ajuta, spuse Poirot.
sentences.translationRu: Насилие вам не поможет, — сказал Пуаро.
sentences.original:
No”—as the doctor
 made a sudden movement—“as a sensible man, you will understand that
 violence will be of no avail. During Hastings’ brief absence to
 fetch you, I have had time to put the bottle in safe keeping. Ah,
 quick, Hastings, hold him!”
 
 I misunderstood Poirot’s anxiety. Eager to save my friend, I flung
 myself in front of him. But the doctor’s swift movement had another
 meaning.

sentences.text: Atunci doctorul a dus mâna la gură.
sentences.translationRu: Тогда доктор поднёс руку ко рту.
sentences.original: His hand went to his mouth,

sentences.text: Apoi a căzut la pământ. El a luat otravă singur.
sentences.translationRu: Потом он упал на землю. Он сам принял яд.
sentences.original:
a smell of bitter almonds
 filled the air, and he swayed forward and fell.

sentences.text: Încă o victimă, dar ultima, spuse Poirot.
sentences.translationRu: Ещё одна жертва, но последняя, — сказал Пуаро.
sentences.original:
“Another victim,” said Poirot gravely, “but the last. Perhaps it
 is the best way. He has three deaths on his head.”

sentences.text: Doctorul Ames? am strigat eu, uimit.
sentences.translationRu: Доктор Эймс? — крикнул я, поражённый.
sentences.original: “Dr. Ames?” I cried, stupefied.

sentences.text: Dar credeam că este un blestem!
sentences.translationRu: Но я думал, что это проклятие!
sentences.original:
“But I thought you believed in some
 occult influence?”

sentences.text: M-ai înțeles greșit, spuse Poirot.
sentences.translationRu: Ты меня не так понял, — сказал Пуаро.
sentences.original: “You misunderstood me, Hastings.

sentences.text: Eu cred în puterea fricii oamenilor.
sentences.translationRu: Я верю в силу человеческого страха.
sentences.original:
What I meant was that I believe
 in the terrific force of superstition.

sentences.text: Când lumea crede într-un blestem, un ucigaș poate omorî ușor.
sentences.translationRu: Когда люди верят в проклятие, убийца может убивать легко.
sentences.original:
Once get it firmly
 established that a series of deaths are supernatural, and you might
 almost stab a man in broad daylight,

sentences.text: Toți vor da vina pe blestem.
sentences.translationRu: Все свалят вину на проклятие.
sentences.original:
and it would still be put down
 to the curse, so strongly is the instinct of the supernatural
 implanted in the human race.

sentences.text: Un om a folosit această frică, și acel om era doctorul Ames.
sentences.translationRu: Один человек использовал этот страх, и этим человеком был доктор Эймс.
sentences.original:
I suspected from the first that a man
 was taking advantage of that instinct. The idea came to him, I
 imagine, with the death of Sir John Willard. A fury of superstition
 arose at once. As far as I could see, nobody could derive any
 particular profit from Sir John’s death. Mr. Bleibner was a
 different case. He was a man of great wealth. The information I
 received from New York contained several suggestive points. To
 begin with, young Bleibner was reported to have said he had a good
 friend in Egypt from whom he could borrow. It was tacitly understood
 that he meant his uncle, but it seemed to me that in that case he
 would have said so outright. The words suggest some boon companion
 of his own. Another thing, he scraped up enough money to take him
 to Egypt, his uncle refused outright to advance him a penny, yet
 he was able to pay the return passage to New York. Some one must
 have lent him the money.”
 
 “All that was very thin,” I objected.
 
 “But there was more. Hastings, there occur often enough words
 spoken metaphorically which are taken literally. The opposite can
 happen too. In this case, words which were meant literally were
 taken metaphorically. Young Bleibner wrote plainly enough: ‘I am
 a leper,’ but nobody realized that he shot himself because he
 believed that he had contracted the dread disease of leprosy.”
 
 “What?” I ejaculated.
 
 “It was the clever invention of a diabolical mind. Young Bleibner
 was suffering from some minor skin trouble, he had lived in the
 South Sea Islands, where the disease is common enough. Ames was a
 former friend of his, and a well-known medical man, he would never
 dream of doubting his word. When I arrived here, my suspicions were
 divided between Harper and Dr. Ames, but I soon realized that only
 the doctor could have perpetrated and concealed the crimes, and I
 learnt from Harper that he was previously acquainted with young
 Bleibner.

sentences.text: De ce a făcut asta? Pentru bani.
sentences.translationRu: Зачем он это сделал? Ради денег.
sentences.original:
Doubtless the latter at some time or another had made a
 will or had insured his life in favour of the doctor. The latter
 saw his chance of acquiring wealth.

sentences.text: Demult, nepotul a scris un testament și a lăsat totul lui Ames.
sentences.translationRu: Когда-то племянник написал завещание и оставил всё Эймсу.

sentences.text: Ames îi salvase odată viața.
sentences.translationRu: Эймс однажды спас ему жизнь.

sentences.text: Ames l-a îmbolnăvit pe unchiul Bleibner, și așa a murit unchiul.
sentences.translationRu: Эймс заразил дядю Блайбнера, и так дядя умер.
sentences.original:
It was easy for him to inoculate
 Mr. Bleibner with the deadly germs. Then the nephew, overcome with
 despair at the dread news his friend had conveyed to him, shot
 himself. Mr. Bleibner, whatever his intentions, had made no will.

sentences.text: Banii treceau de la unchi la nepot, și de la nepot la doctor.
sentences.translationRu: Деньги переходили от дяди к племяннику, а от племянника к доктору.
sentences.original: His fortune would pass to his nephew and from him to the doctor.”

sentences.text: Și de ce a murit nepotul?
sentences.translationRu: А отчего умер племянник?
sentences.original: “And Mr. Schneider?”

sentences.text: Ames i-a spus o minciună crudă: Tu ești bolnav de lepră.
sentences.translationRu: Эймс сказал ему жестокую ложь: Ты болен проказой.
sentences.original: “We cannot be sure. He knew young Bleibner too, remember,

sentences.text: Nepotul a crezut că este lepros, și de spaimă s-a omorât.
sentences.translationRu: Племянник поверил, что он прокажённый, и от ужаса убил себя.
sentences.original:
and may
 have suspected something, or, again,

sentences.text: Și domnul Schneider? am întrebat eu.
sentences.translationRu: А мистер Шнайдер? — спросил я.
sentences.original:
the doctor may have thought
 that a further death motiveless and purposeless would strengthen
 the coils of superstition.

sentences.text: Poate a bănuit ceva, spuse Poirot.
sentences.translationRu: Может, он что-то заподозрил, — сказал Пуаро.
sentences.original:
Furthermore, I will tell you an
 interesting psychological fact, Hastings.

sentences.text: Sau Ames a vrut mai multă frică.
sentences.translationRu: Или Эймс хотел больше страха.

sentences.text: Un ucigaș vrea adesea să repete crima.
sentences.translationRu: Убийца часто хочет повторить преступление.
sentences.original:
A murderer has always a
 strong desire to repeat his successful crime, the performance of
 it grows upon him. Hence my fears for young Willard.

sentences.text: Și umbra cu cap de câine era Hassan.
sentences.translationRu: А тень с собачьей головой была Хассаном.
sentences.original:
The figure of
 Anubis you saw to-night was Hassan,

sentences.text: Eu l-am rugat să se îmbrace ca Anubis.
sentences.translationRu: Я попросил его нарядиться Анубисом.
sentences.original: dressed up by my orders.

sentences.text: Am vrut să-l sperii pe doctor.
sentences.translationRu: Я хотел напугать доктора.
sentences.original:
I
 wanted to see if I could frighten the doctor. But it would take
 more than the supernatural to frighten him. I could see that he
 was not entirely taken in by my pretences of belief in the occult.
 The little comedy I played for him did not deceive him.

sentences.text: Apoi am știut că el vrea să mă omoare.
sentences.translationRu: Потом я понял, что он хочет убить меня.
sentences.original:
I suspected
 that he would endeavour to make me the next victim. Ah, but in
 spite of la mer maudite, the heat abominable, and the annoyances
 of the sand, the little grey cells still functioned!”

sentences.text: Poirot a avut dreptate.
sentences.translationRu: Пуаро был прав.
sentences.original: Poirot proved to be perfectly right in his premises.

sentences.text: Sir Guy Willard era acum în siguranță.
sentences.translationRu: Сэр Гай Уиллард был теперь в безопасности.
sentences.original:
Young Bleibner,
 some years ago, in a fit of drunken merriment, had made a jocular
 will, leaving “my cigarette case you admire so much and everything
 else of which I die possessed which will be principally debts to
 my good friend Robert Ames who once saved my life from drowning.”

sentences.text: Lumea încă vorbește despre blestem.
sentences.translationRu: Люди всё ещё говорят о проклятии.
sentences.original:
The case was hushed up as far as possible, and, to this day, people
 talk of the remarkable series of deaths in connection with the Tomb
 of Men-her-Ra as a triumphal proof of the vengeance of a bygone
 king upon the desecrators of his tomb—

sentences.text: Dar nu a fost niciun blestem, ci doar un om și lăcomia lui.
sentences.translationRu: Но проклятия не было, был лишь человек и его жадность.
sentences.original:
a belief which, as Poirot
 pointed out to me, is contrary to all Egyptian belief and thought.
 
 
 
 
 VII
